contribution_summary: Formally creates ILM and drives the production pressure under which the Van Nuys model and effects program is built.
Locates the Van Nuys warehouse, builds the effects unit, and dates the Dykstraflex design to July 1975.
Part of the leadership layer bridging Lucasfilm production and the special photographic effects unit.
Named by Dykstra in the Dykstraflex design and build effort, tying him to how miniatures were filmed rather than to one specific ship.

contribution_summary: Sets up the art department, helps produce early boards, signs the 9/25/75 Escape Pod blueprint, and is repeatedly linked to early X-wing and likely early Y-wing development.
Starts on boards with Johnston, then is explicitly tied to the Y-wing Number One master pattern, the Death Star cannon with Stuart Ziff, and the Sandcrawler as a broad team build.

linked_models_or_systems: X-Wing; Blockade Runner; broader ship program
contribution_summary: Early core hire; directly named in the X-wing filming-design phase and later tied by provenance trails to Blockade Runner material.
Explicitly named in ILM's retrospective as part of the early buildable X-wing development phase.
Explicitly named in the early X-wing development cohort as the shop moves from concept to shootable miniatures.
Explicitly named in the early X-wing filming-design phase.
One of the builders specifically credited on the first completed ILM X-wing before it was shipped to England.

Early core hire and later provenance anchor for opening-shot Blockade Runner material; more securely tied to how miniatures were filmed than to any one specific build job.
Early hire on the animation / optical side of turning miniature elements into finished shots.
Early hire on the animation side, later recalled by Dave Brown as part of the Rotoscope & Animation group.
Internal witness for staffing timing, department flow, and the final 24/7 optical crunch; his footage also preserves the daily Van Nuys environment.
